description_long: This object consists of three distinct lanterns, each with unique designs and colors. The left lantern is a dark brown, pagoda-style structure with a tiered roof and a solid pedestal base, reminiscent of traditional Japanese garden lanterns. The central lantern is metallic gray, featuring a classic cylindrical body with a rounded top and bottom, flanked by two curved handles and a central ring, evoking the look of an old-fashioned oil lamp. The right lantern is bright red with a square base and roof, open sides, and a gold ring handle on top, resembling a portable Asian lantern. All three lanterns are constructed with hard, smooth surfaces, likely metal, and are designed to be both decorative and functional in urban outdoor settings. The lanterns vary in height and width, but all are compact enough to be portable or mounted on posts or tables. Their surfaces are mostly matte or slightly glossy, with minimal decorative embellishments.
description: A group of three lanterns: a brown pagoda-style, a gray classic oil lamp, and a red square lantern with a gold handle, each with smooth, hard surfaces and distinct urban decorative styles.
description_view_0: Front view shows the brown pagoda lantern in the foreground, with the gray and red lanterns partially visible behind it.
description_view_1: Side view displays all three lanterns clearly: the brown pagoda on the left, the gray oil lamp in the center, and the red square lantern with a gold handle on the right.
description_view_2: Rear view aligns the lanterns in a row, showing the backs and tops, with the gold handle of the red lantern most prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, with the red lantern on the left, the gray oil lamp in the center, and the brown pagoda on the right.
